recentEvaluations.tweetText:
If you aren't yet bold enough to install the Codex app, you can stay in the presence of your orange crab and point it at GPT 5.6 Sol. Takes 5 minutes. Kudos to Theo for explaining one of the ways to get this done.

Step 1: Install CLIProxyAPI
Step 2: Connect
Step 3: Define following alias and enjoy claudex

```
alias claudex='CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=gpt-5.6-sol \
CLAUDE_CODE_ALWAYS_ENABLE_EFFORT=1 \
CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TOOL_USE_CONCURRENCY=3 \
ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=false \
claude --model gpt-5.6-sol'
```

If this gets blocked, I owe you a reset.

If you were using Codex before, two things to do

1. Keep using Codex. It is for you
2. Tell your friends and family about ChatGPT Work who you always wanted to show Codex but they’re not as technical and don’t work with code every day

automationEvents.rationale: The tweet explicitly says resets were applied automatically, indicating limits were reset now.
automationEvents.tweetText:
We applied the resets automatically because of two reasons.

1. It is not possible to use a banked reset from the web or mobile ChatGPT. We are launching it next week and it will require a bit of education for all our new ChatGPT Work users.
2. The system is under a lot of pressure and full resets are more predictable in how they affect load on the infrastructure. This helps us keeping things stable in the first 48 hours of the launch.

And we will use banked resets once we are back in the normal operating cadence!
